english: *daṅ saṅ - te med* (dang sang te med) W. (the soot) having yesterday been removed, there is none just now; *saṅ dug, saṅ ćos* (sang dug, sang chos) W. it is cleansed, swept clean, *bag saṅ, nyé-pa saṅ* (bag sang, nye pa sang) the contamination, the sin, has been removed, done away with C.; snyun saṅs (snyun sangs) the disease is removed Pth.; skyo-saṅs byéd-pa (skyo sangs byed pa) to remove melancholy, to recreate or amuse one's self; to comfort others; skyo-saṅs-la ₒgró-ba (skyo sangs la 'gro ba), skyo-saṅs byéd-pa (skyo sangs byed pa) to take a walk, to take a ride Pth., C.; mya-ṅaṅ sáṅ-ba (mya ngan sang ba) to comfort Pth., to console one's self; esp. 2. to recover, to come again to one's senses, ra-ro-ba-las (ra ro ba las) from intoxication Dzl.; γzím-pa-las (gzim pa las) from a deep sleep Dzl.; also construed as before: bzi (bzi) Glr., *ra* (ra) W. from a drunken fit, and this agrees with a sufficiently authenticated signification of the Ssk. root budh, so that saṅs-rgyás (sangs rgyas) would after all be the literal translation of बुद्ध (contrary to Burn. I, 71 med.), taking the signification of the name, accord. to Tibetan notions, to be: 'the man that has entirely recovered from error and come to the knowledge of absolute truth'. That saṅs-rgyás (sangs rgyas) be the same as perfect, holy, seems to be a mere etymological conjecture of Cs. — 3. to take away, to take off, *keb saṅ-wa* (keb sang wa) C. to uncover. — 4. to be spoiled, to become unfit, useless, *wó-ma saṅ soṅ* ('o ma sang song) C. the milk is spoiled, zom saṅ ₒdug (zom sang 'dug) = saṅ ₒdug (sang 'dug) the casks are leaky, are running out.
Sch.: the neck of the thigh-bone; ₒtsaṅ-rai tsil ('tshang rai tshil) the fat attached to it C.
pf. tsabs (tshabs), bsabs (bsabs), fut. bsab (bsab), imp. tsob (tshob), to pay back, repay, refund, skyin-pa (skyin pa) a loan Lex.; cf. tsab (tshab).
hurry, confusion, perplexity, fear Sch.; also: ₒtsab-ₒtsáb-mor gnás-pa ('tshab 'tshab mor gnas pa) to tarry in fear, to hesitate in apprehensions Tar.
pf. tsabs (tshabs), imp. tsobs (tshobs) Sch.: resp. to be afraid; Lex. blo-ₒtsábs (blo 'tshabs) id. (?).

1. = ₒćam-pa ('cham pa) (?) fit, suitable, in accordance to, in conformity with, de daṅ ₒtsám-par (de dang 'tsham par) S.g.; so-sói ₒbyór-pa daṅ ₒtsám-par (so so'i 'byor pa dang 'tsham par) Tar. according to their ability, in proportion to their property. — 2. frq. and mostly erron. for mtsáms-pa (mtshams pa).

pf. tsar (tshar) 1. to be finished, completed, terminated, sṅón-la tsár-ro (sngon la tshar ro) Glr. it was the first that was finished; to be at an end, consumed, spent, *nor tsár-te soṅ* (nor tshar te song) W. the money is all spent; esp. as an auxiliary, to denote an action that is perfectly past or completed (where in the earlier literature zin (zin) stands), in later books with the termin. inf., yóṅs-su rdzógs-par tsár-te (yongs su rdzogs par tshar te) when... was completely finished Glr.; vulgo the mere root is used, esp. in W., *tsog tsar-ra ma tsar* (tshog tshar ra ma tshar) are they assembled, has the meeting begun already? *lam-la źug tsar, soṅ tsar, kal tsar* (lam la zhug tshar, song tshar, kal tshar) he is on the way, he is gone, it is dispatched; tsár-ba byéd-pa (tshar ba byed pa), tsár-du ₒjúg-pa (tshar du 'jug pa) Cs., *tsar ćug-ċe* (tshar chug ce) W. to bring to a close, to finish, to terminate. — tsár-γċod-pa (tshar gcod pa) 1. to destroy, annihilate, e.g. diabolic influences, infernal powers Pth.; to defeat, overcome, in disputation Mil.; to excel, surpass, sgyu-rtsál-gyis (sgyu rtsal gyis) Glr.; to punish Tar. 2. for gsár-γċod-pa (gsar gcod pa) Pth. — 2. to grow, grow up, thrive, of little children W.; ₒtsar-skyéd ('tshar skyed) growth Mil.
sgro-bai-ₒtsal-gyi ka-brgyan (sgro ba'i 'tshal gyi ka brgyan) Mil.?

imp. ₒtsol ('tshol) eleg. 1. to want, wish, desire, ask; when followed by a verb, the latter stands in the termin. inf., or the mere root of it, and more esp. that of the perf. form, yab daṅ mjal ₒtsál-lo (yab dang mjal 'tshal lo) I have a mind to go to see my father Dzl.; bltás-par ₒtsál-te (bltas par 'tshal te) wishing to see Dzl.; túgs-la bżag ₒtsal (thugs la bzhag 'tshal) I wish it may be borne in mind Glr.; gsuṅ ₒtsal (gsung 'tshal) I beg you to speak Mil., bzuṅ ₒtsal (bzung 'tshal) please take Pth.; pleon. ₒkhríd-par żu ₒtsal ('khrid par zhu 'tshal) Glr.; esp. as an intimation of willingness, dé-ltar ₒtsál-lo (de ltar 'tshal lo) yes, we will do that Mil., or like our: very well! Further: pá-la nor ma ₒtsál-tam (pha la nor ma 'tshal tam) has he not asked the money from his father? Dzl.; gum yaṅ ċi ₒtsál (gum yang ci 'tshal) why does (the king) want to kill me? Dzl.; dëi don mi ₒtsal (de'i don mi 'tshal) the profit of it I do not desire Glr. — 2. to eat, btsan-dúg (btsan dug)
